entities.doc: CompositeOrganizaiton is a type of Organization that is formed by aggregating other Organizations, which may be atomic or composite Organizations.

entities.doc: A feature or quality used to make recognizable or to define somebody or something, such as age, income, education, revenue, and so forth.

entities.name: CommunityAdministrator
entities.doc: A CommunityAdministrator is a PartyRole, played by a Party, who administers a Community.

entities.doc: A CommunityMember is a PartyRole, played by an Individual, who is committing to a Community, contributes to it and consumed Assets from the Community.

entities.name: Contribution
entities.doc: A Contribution is a BusinessInteraction, containing items, associated to Resource, Service or Product that is contributed to a Community by a CommunityMember.

entities.name: ContributionProviderRole
entities.doc: A ContributionProviderRole is a BusinessInteractionRole a party (identified by a CommunityMember) plays when contributing to a Community.

entities.name: ContributionReceiverRole
entities.doc: A ContributioReceiverRole is a BusinessInteractionRole a party (identified by a Community) plays when receiving a contribution from a CommunityMember.

entities.name: AddressContactMediumRole
entities.doc: A ContactMedium, such as a telephone number or fax number, associated with an address.

entities.name: Place
entities.doc: The time period that the Place is valid for Permitted Values: Refer Time Period class

entities.name: PlacePhysicalResourceAssoc
entities.doc: The association between a PhysicalResource and a Place (Location).

entities.name: AdministrativeArea
entities.doc: Administrative Area represents the many areas that are controlled or managed by an Administration. Administrations could include councils, governments … This is an example of a concrete Geographic Loc

entities.name: Country
entities.doc: This is an example of a concrete Geographic Location entity.

entities.name: ExchangeLocation
entities.doc: A geographic loaction, typically a building, used to house the inside plant equipment of potentially several telephone exchanges, each serving a certain geographical exchange area.

entities.name: ExchangeServiceArea
entities.doc: A region or part of a town, a country, or the world that is served by an exchange.

entities.name: GeographicArea
entities.doc: This is a generic entity, used to represent areas defined on maps that relate to areas of settlement.This is an example of a concrete Geographic Location entity.

entities.name: GeographicLocation
entities.doc: A defined place on or near the earth's surface. It is an abstract modeling concept that provides a linking point to other parts of the SID model.It holds attributes common to all Geographic Location s

entities.name: GeographicLocationName
entities.doc: A word, term, or phrase by which a Geographic Location is known and distinguished from other Geographic Locations. A name is an informal way of identifying an object [Fowler]Separating Location Name f

entities.name: GeographicLocationRelationship
entities.doc: This association allows us to show direct relationships between Geographic Locations.This should be used with care to ensure that the association reflects reality

entities.name: GeographicLocationType
entities.doc: This is a simple specification entity used to provide a list of allowable geographic location types. These types correspond to the concrete entity names shown on fig 10a

entities.name: GeographicPlace
entities.doc: Allows us to determine where things are in relation to the earth's surface. It is an abstract modeling concept that provides a linking point to other parts of the SID model.

entities.name: GeographicPostcode
entities.doc: A postal delivery area used to speed and simplify the delivery of mail.

entities.name: GeographicSite
entities.doc: A convenience class that allows us to easily refer to Places important to the Service Provider.

entities.name: GeographicSiteRole
entities.doc: The part played by a Site in a given context with any characteristics, such as expected pattern of behavior, attributes, and/or associations that it entails.

entities.name: GeographicState
entities.doc: This is an example of a concrete Geographic Location entity.It can be used to represent States and their equivalents.

entities.name: Jurisdiction
entities.doc: An area over which authority extends.

entities.name: LandUseType
entities.doc: The kind of land use, such as the kinds of human uses of land.

entities.name: NetworkRoute
entities.doc: A way or course taken in getting from a starting point to a destination in a network.

entities.name: NetworkRouteSection
entities.doc: A component of a NetworkRoute.

entities.name: Property
entities.doc: A piece of land with defined legal boundaries.This is an example of a concrete Geographic Location entity. Synonyms / Aliases: Land Parcel

entities.name: SimpleGeographicLocation
entities.doc: A GeographicLocation that is composed of a single element; not compound.

entities.name: Street
entities.doc: A public or private road in a city or town, typically with houses and buildings on one or both sides.

entities.name: StreetName
entities.doc: The name of the street or other street type.

entities.name: StreetSegment
entities.doc: Each of the parts into which Street is or may be divided.

entities.name: StreetSegmentGeoAddressRelationship
entities.doc: The relationship between a StreetSegment and a GeographicAddress.

entities.name: AbstractGeographicAddress
entities.doc: An abstract (not a material) address from which others, such as GeogrphicAddress, inherit.

entities.name: AmericanPropertyAddress
entities.doc: A type of urban property address that represents an American address. Note: This is an example entity.

entities.name: AustralianPropertyAddress
entities.doc: A type of urban property address that represents an Australian address. Note: This is an example entity.

entities.name: AustralianRuralAddress
entities.doc: A type of urban property address that represents a rural Australian address. Note: This is an example entity.

entities.name: GeographicAddress
entities.doc: A structured textual way of describing how to find a Geographic Location. It is usually composed of an ordered list of Geographic Location names based on context specific rulesIt is an abstract modeli

entities.name: GeographicSubAddress
entities.doc: A Geographic Sub Address "contains information in addition to that generally found in a standard single address [ANZLIC-STREET]It is an abstract modeling concept that provides a linking point to other

entities.name: JapanesePropertyAddress
entities.doc: A type of urban property address that represents a Japanese address. Note: This is an example entity.

entities.name: LogicalAddress
entities.doc: An address that allows sending something (tangible or intangible) to a central point where it can be retrieved. For example, with email, a message is sent to the recipient’s ISP mail server. The recip

entities.name: PoBoxAddress
entities.doc: An optional component of a PostalDeliveryAddress that reprsents a post office box number.

entities.name: PostalDeliveryAddress
entities.doc: The address to which posts (mail) are delivered.

entities.name: PropertyAddressAssociation
entities.doc: An association between Property and UrbanPropertyAddress that provides the address of the Property.

entities.name: PropertySubAddress
entities.doc: An type of address used for addressing within a property. It may refer to a building, a building cluster, or a floor of a multistory building.

entities.name: UrbanPropertyAddress
entities.doc: An UrbanPropertyAddress is a structured textual way of describing how to find a Property in an urban area (country properties are often defined differently). It is usually composed of an ordered list

entities.name: UrbanPropertySubAddress
entities.doc: An UrbanPropertySubAddress is used for addressing within a property in an urban area (country properties are often defined differently).. It may refer to a building, a building cluster, or a floor of

abes.notes: GIS map symbology is out of BSS/OSS scope for this stack

entities.name: LineSymbology
entities.doc: A symbol that represents a line.

entities.name: PointSymbology
entities.doc: A symbol represented as a point.

entities.name: SurfaceSymbology
entities.doc: A symbol that represents a surface (any figure having only two dimensions).

entities.name: Symbology
entities.doc: An abstract object used to represents something.

entities.name: AbsoluteLocalLocation
entities.doc: A LocalLocation that is defined using Cartesian coordinates that relate back to a LocalCoordinateSystem. The coordinate units are defined in the LocalCoordinateSystem.

entities.name: AbsoluteLocalOrientation
entities.doc: The relative position of something measured independently and not in relation to something else.

entities.name: CompoundInternalLocation
entities.doc: A group of measurements and relationships of lines, angles, surfaces, solids and points used to display internal locations.

entities.name: CompoundLocalAddress
entities.doc: A group of LocalAddresses.

entities.name: GridLocalLocation
entities.doc: A LocalLocation that is defined using Grid coordinates that relate back to a LocalGridCoordinateSystem This would be used when locations are not defined in inches or millimeters but rather in terms of

entities.name: LinearReferenceSystem
entities.doc: A method of spatial referencing, in which the locations of features are described in terms of measurements along a linear element, from a defined starting point, for example a milestone along a road.

entities.name: LocalContinuativeCoordSystem
entities.doc: A coordinate system for LocalLocations that are defined using floating point Cartesian coordinates

entities.name: LocalContinuativeLocation
entities.doc: This is an abstract modeling concept that provides a linking point to other parts of the SID model. Its subclasses are entities that are locations that are defined using floating point Cartesian coord

entities.name: LocalGeometry
entities.doc: A measurement and relationship of lines, angles, surfaces, solids and points used for the display of LocalLocations.

entities.name: LocalGridCoordinateSystem
entities.doc: A coordinate system for LocalLocations that are defined using Grid coordinates

entities.name: LocalLineString
entities.doc: A set of coordinate points and the lines that join them for LocalLocations

entities.name: LocalLocation
entities.doc: A defined place defined by a local coordinate system. It is an abstract modeling concept that provides a linking point to other parts of the SID model. It holds attributes common to all Local Location

entities.name: LocalLocationRelationship
entities.doc: An association between two LocalLocations.

entities.name: LocalPlace
entities.doc: Allows us to determine where things are in relation to a local coordinate system.It is an abstract modeling concept that provides a linking point to other parts of the SID model.

entities.name: LocalPoint
entities.doc: A particular spot, place, or position in an area or on a map, object, or surface used for LocalPlaces.

entities.name: LocalPolygon
entities.doc: A plane figure with at least three straight sides and angles, and typically five or more used for LocalPlaces.

entities.name: RelativeLocalLocation
entities.doc: A LocalLocation that is defined using coordinates that are relative to another LocalLocation. The coordinate units are defined in the LocalCoordinateSystem.

entities.name: Representation
entities.doc: A very general relationship that expresses similarities between objects.

entities.name: SimpleLocalAddress
entities.doc: The lowest level, or atomic, LocalAddress. It may exist alone or be part of a CompoundLocalAddress.

entities.name: SolidRepresentation
entities.doc: A type of Representation that is firm and stable in shape, i.e. solid.

entities.name: WireFrameRepresentation
entities.doc: This is an example Resource related entity used to show the representation of a wire frame type of resource.

abes.name: OpenGisSFS ABE
abes.notes: OpenGIS simple-feature geometry classes are out of scope; geo data is stored as plain address/coordinate fields

entities.name: Curve
entities.doc: A line or outline that gradually deviates from being straight for some or all of its length.

entities.name: Geometry
entities.doc: A measurement and relationship of lines, angles, surfaces, solids and points.

entities.name: GeometryCollection
entities.doc: A group of Geometries.

entities.name: Line
entities.doc: A mark connecting two points.

entities.name: LinearRing
entities.doc: A LineString which is both closed and simple. In other words, the first and last coordinate in the ring must be equal, and the interior of the ring must not self-intersect.

entities.name: LineString
entities.doc: A set of coordinate points and the lines that join them.

entities.name: MultiCurve
entities.doc: A geometry collection composed of Curve elements.

entities.name: MultiLineString
entities.doc: A representation of multiple LineStrings.

entities.name: MultiPoint
entities.doc: A composition of more than one Point.

entities.name: MultiPolygon
entities.doc: A composition of more than one Polygon.

entities.name: MultiSurface
entities.doc: A composition of more than one Surface.

entities.name: Point
entities.doc: A particular spot, place, or position in an area or on a map, object, or surface.

entities.name: Polygon
entities.doc: A plane figure with at least three straight sides and angles, and typically five or more.

entities.name: SpatialReferenceSystem
entities.doc: A spatial reference system (SRS) or coordinate reference system (CRS) is a coordinate-based local, regional or global system used to locate geographical entities. A spatial reference system defines a

entities.name: Surface
entities.doc: A continuous set of points that has length and breadth but no thickness.

entities.doc: A CommunicationInteractionItem is the smallest entity in a CommunicationInteraction tree. It concerns a unique CommunicationInteractionUseCase and involves the same CommunicationCommunicationInteracti

entities.name: CommunicationInteraction
entities.doc: A CommunicationInteraction is a kind of action that occur as two or more objects have an effect upon one another (wikipedia). More precisely, in SID, a CommunicationInteraction describes an exchange o

entities.name: Email
entities.doc: Electronic mail, commonly called email or e-mail, is a method of exchanging digital messages across the Internet or other computer networks.

entities.name: TVAdvertising
entities.doc: A TVAdvertising tracks advertising published through TV channel.

entities.name: CommunicationInteractionMeans
entities.doc: A CommunicationInteractionMeans represents the content of A CommunicationInteraction such as email, phonecall, SMS, MMS…

entities.name: CommunicationInteractionAction
entities.doc: A CommunicationInteractionAction specifies which action is done on a RootEntity in the context of a CommunicationInteractionUseCase or CommunicationInteractionResult.

entities.name: CommunicationInteractionResult
entities.doc: A CommunicationInteractionResult specifies describes the main result which entity (RootEntity) is impacted and for which action (CommunicationInteractionAction) by the CommunicationInteractionItem. Fo

entities.name: CommunicationInteractionUseCase
entities.doc: A CommunicationInteractionUseCase specifies which entity (RootEntity) is concerned and which action (CommunicationInteractionAction) are concerned by the CommunicationInteractionItem. For example: - A

entities.name: CommunicationInteractionRole
entities.doc: A CommunicationInteractionRole specifies which roles (PartyRole or ResourceRole) are involved in a CommunicationInteractionItem.

entities.name: MMS
entities.doc: Multimedia Messaging Service tracked.

entities.name: SMS
entities.doc: Short Message Service tracked.

entities.name: PhoneCall
entities.doc: A PhoneCall tracks a call using TelephoneNumber Contact Medium.

entities.name: FaceToFace
entities.doc: A FaceToFace tracks a physical meeting between people. It might have happened according to an Appointment or not.

entities.name: Mail
entities.doc: A mail is a method of exchanging physically postcards, letters and parcels.

entities.name: CalendarEntryParticipation
entities.doc: Shows the participation for a CalendarEntry

entities.name: CalendarEntryAssociation
entities.doc: Allows us to associate Calendar Entries

entities.name: TemporalExpression
entities.doc: An expression that defines one or more time periods, often periodic in nature. This is an abstract entity that abstracts simple and recurring time periods from the calendar entry. It may also allow re

entities.name: SingleAttendee
entities.doc: DO NOT IMPLEMENT

entities.name: SimpleTemporalExpression
entities.doc: This is used when a Calendar Entry has a simple single start and end DateTime.

entities.name: RelativeCalendarAlarm
entities.doc: A CalendarAlarm that is triggered relative to the start or end of a CalendarEntry

entities.name: RecurrenceTemporalExpression
entities.doc: This is a placeholder entity that would likely be implemented using a rule or policy engine. Refer to [iCalendar] for an example implementation via recurrence and exception Rules.

entities.name: CalendarToDo
entities.doc: This represents 'an action-item or assignment. For example, it can be used to represent an item of work assigned to an individual; such as "turn in travel expense today".'

entities.name: CalendarPartyRole
entities.doc: DO NOT IMPLEMENT

entities.name: CalendarJournalEntry
entities.doc: Represents "one or more descriptive text notes associated with a particular calendar date" [iCalendar]

entities.name: CalendarEvent
entities.doc: "represents a scheduled amount of time on a calendar. For example, it can be an activity; such as a one-hour long, department meeting from 8:00 AM to 9:00 AM, tomorrow. Generally, an event will take u

entities.name: CalendarEntryOrganizer
entities.doc: DO NOT IMPLEMENT

entities.name: CalendarEntryAttendee
entities.doc: DO NOT IMPLEMENT

entities.name: CalendarEntry
entities.doc: An entry in a calendar. This is an abstract entity.

entities.name: CalendarAlarm
entities.doc: Is "a reminder or alarm for an event or a to-do. For example, it may be used to define a reminder for a pending event or an overdue to-do." [iCalendar]

entities.name: CalendarActionEntry
entities.doc: This is an abstract entity that contains common properties and associationsfor CalendarEvents & ToDos

entities.name: Calendar
entities.doc: A Calendar is "a collection of calendaring and scheduling information" [iCalendar]

entities.name: AttendeeGroup
entities.doc: DO NOT IMPLEMENT

entities.name: AbsoluteCalendarAlarm
entities.doc: A Calendar Alarm that is triggered at an absolute time.

entities.name: Appointment
entities.doc: An Appointment is an arrangement to do something or meet someone at a particular time and location.

entities.name: DateScheduleDefinition
entities.doc: The date schedule is used to define a schedule that is based on specific dates, such as December 31st 2015, Feberaury 28th 2013, etc.

entities.name: FixedScheduleDefinition
entities.doc: The FixedScheduleDefinition entity holds ta list of fixed executions in the sense that each execution has specific time parameters (i.e. starts at a certain date & time).

entities.name: FixedScheduleItem
entities.doc: The FixedScheduleItem entity holds a list of specific parameters (e.g. start date & time) related to a single execution .

entities.name: MonthlyScheduleDayOfMonthDefinition
entities.doc: The monthly schedule by days of month is used to define a schedule that is based on specifying which days of month are requited, e.g. the10th, 20th, 30th of each month.

entities.name: MonthlyScheduleDayOfWeekDefinition
entities.doc: The monthly schedule by days of week is used to define a monthly schedule based on the sequnce of day in a month by specifying its instance, e.g. the 1st Monday of the month.

entities.name: RecurringScheduleDefinition
entities.doc: The RecurringScheduleDefinition entity holds the parameters related to a schedule that defines recurrent executions.

entities.name: ScheduledDuration
entities.doc: The Scheduled Duration entity defines a set of parameters related to the duration of a job/activity, such as the planned base duration and possible extensions.

entities.name: ScheduleDefinition
entities.doc: The Schedule Definition base Entity holds the required parameters for the definition of a schedule that will be attached to jobs.

entities.name: ScheduleDefinitionExcludedDates
entities.doc: Excluded dates are dates that should be excluded from the Schedule definition, e.g. the schedule definition will not be active on May 1st 2014.

entities.name: WeeklyScheduleDefinition
entities.doc: The weekly schedule is used to define a schedule that is based on the days of the week, e.g. a schedule that will be active only on Monday, Tuesday.

entities.name: PaymentPlanPaymentMethod
entities.doc: A PaymentPlanPaymentMethod specifies the priority and valid period for each PaymentMethod in a PaymentPlan context.

entities.name: ThirdPartyPaymentAgency
entities.doc: ThirdPartyPayeeAgency is a third party between SP and party. This agency takes a role of receiving payment for SP from SP's party.

entities.name: PaymentPlan
entities.doc: PaymentPlan defines the plan for payment via its associations to the PaymentMethod and PaymentAccount.

entities.name: PartyPayment
entities.doc: The PartyPayment represents the transfer of wealth from any Party to another one.

entities.name: AccountBalancePM
entities.doc: AccountBalancePM defines possible payment method using BillingAccountBalance as payment source.

entities.name: BankCardPM
entities.doc: PaymentMethod used to pay with a bank card.

entities.name: CashPM
entities.doc: CashPM is an example of payment method, through which payment can be made by cash.

entities.name: CheckPM
entities.doc: Pay with a check.

entities.name: CreditCardPM
entities.doc: CreditCardPM is an example of payment method, using which payment can be made by credit card.

entities.name: DebitCardPM
entities.doc: Pay with a debit card issued by a bank.

entities.name: DigitalWalletPM
entities.doc: A DigitalWalletPM enables debit on a digital wallet.

entities.name: LoyaltyBurnPM
entities.doc: A payment method that uses loyalty points to make a payment.

entities.name: PaymentMethod
entities.doc: PaymentMethod is the way in which party provides payment, for example cash ,credit,transfer.

entities.name: PaymentMethodSpecification
entities.doc: A PaymentMethodSpecification defines characteristics that mighnt be used to describe a PaymentMethod.

entities.name: ThirdPartyCollectionPM
entities.doc: ThirdPartyCollectionPM is an example of payment method, using which payment can be made through the third party,such as bank transfer, payment tool(like PayPal, Alipay).

entities.name: Bank
entities.doc: Represent the general bank.

entities.name: BankAccount
entities.doc: Represent the general bank account.
